name: the-broken-ladder-how-inequality-affects-the-way-we-think-live-and-die
description: Keith Payne's The Broken Ladder — how economic inequality rewires our brains, distorts our decisions, and shortens our lives.
useCase: Understand how inequality affects psychology, health, decisions, and politics — and learn how to escape the comparison trap.
knownRisks: Content discusses sensitive topics including racial bias and police violence
Some readers may find descriptions of inequality stress-inducing
Solutions are primarily individual-level; structural change requires collective action
